title: vLLM proposes “micro-agents” inside the model-serving layer
friendlyTitle: vLLM shows a new way to make AI answers better
summary: The vLLM team argues that routers can become capability-building layers, turning a single OpenAI-compatible API call into bounded collaboration among multiple models, roles, verifiers, and synthesis steps. Their Semantic Router patterns include escalation, parallel ratings, repeated mixture-of-model reasoning, and disagreement-based fusion.
friendlySummary: vLLM describes a system where one AI request can quietly use several models or checks behind the scenes, then return one clean answer.
whyItMatters: If this pattern matures, teams may get stronger results without rewriting every app as a custom agent graph. It points toward a future where orchestration, verification, and fallback live in the serving layer behind a normal model endpoint.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could make AI tools more reliable without forcing every developer to build a complicated agent system from scratch.
